title: podman inspect on stopped container: inconsistent status
body:
At some recent point, podman inspect started reporting 'stopped' for nonroot and 'exited' for root:

```
$ podman run -d --name foo alpine sleep 60;podman stop foo;podman inspect --format '{{.State.Status}}' foo
003cdf7dc49ebaff3261c05ed1cc928e26383f5b065b5f835b30a1e909aaffbd
003cdf7dc49ebaff3261c05ed1cc928e26383f5b065b5f835b30a1e909aaffbd
stopped
```

```
# podman run -d --name foo alpine sleep 60;podman stop foo;podman inspect --format '{{.State.Status}}' foo
2a5a082ef5dee2babb0ef4597984f640dd5e35194788c21f025399ca792e9991
2a5a082ef5dee2babb0ef4597984f640dd5e35194788c21f025399ca792e9991
exited
```

Caught by BATS tests (which expect, and have long expected, 'exited')

comments.body: We might have also broken `--syslog` at some point, which was the only way of debugging these things, so that's fun

It's mount options - the PR from @rhatdan that disabled mount options if a new storage driver was specified.

It's wiping the fuse-overlayfs path so it tries to use kernel overlayfs.
Wow. Thanks for identifying. This has been a surreal gremliny week, it's comforting to know that there's a logical explanation for this one.
